title: Quick Connect - No Hostname Specified!
author: ivantiglao

iterations.description: The `CreateQuickConnect` method does not validate if the provided `connectionString` (hostname) is null or whitespace, leading to the creation of an invalid `ConnectionInfo` object.

comments.author: nmat
comments.snippet: Does the problem happen on if you use IP also?

comments.author: Spo-oky
comments.snippet:
Same here...
Using name or IP appears "Cannot open connection: No Hostname specified!"

I'm using Windows 10 and the portable version.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The `CreateQuickConnect` method does not validate if the provided `connectionString` (hostname) is null or whitespace, leading to the creation of an invalid `ConnectionInfo` object.
Approach: Add a `string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace` check at the beginning of `CreateQuickConnect` to return `null` early if the `connectionString` is invalid, and add a corresponding error message to the language resource file.
